cross_asset_summaries.dollar_commodities: 油價再度引發通膨風險，美元走強、黃金走弱則強化實質殖利率衝擊。

header.stance: 能源價格與 Fed 鷹派重新定價推升實質殖利率，美債走弱，融資情勢仍有序

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 開盤固定收益論點

7 月 13 日美債收盤延續前次最終收盤以來的限制性利率環境，但能源在 7 月 10 日走勢中影響消退後，再度成為直接催化劑。美國與伊朗再度交火，加上擬對通過荷姆茲海峽的貨物收費，使燃料價格衝擊的威脅再起。Christopher Waller 隨後把另一份偏熱的核心通膨數據與近期可能緊縮連結起來，進一步放大這項衝擊。2年期殖利率收於 4.26%，10 年期收於 4.62%，顯示這波賣壓的核心是預期政策路徑上移，而非資金湧向避險資產。

3條管道主導了這波走勢。油價提高未來整體通膨的推升力道，使回歸物價穩定的路徑更加複雜。Waller 強調，在最新能源衝擊之前，基礎通膨便已加速，因此交易員不能將此波走勢視為純粹的地緣政治溢價。貨幣市場將 7 月升息的隱含機率從盤中稍早低於 40% 上調至接近 50%，使前端與殖利率曲線腹部對下一份通膨數據及主席 Kevin Warsh的國會證詞格外敏感。

名目債券的賣壓也要求更高的實質補償。10 年期實質殖利率收於 2.36%，通膨補償的升幅則小於名目殖利率。即使新一波油價衝擊帶來更明確的通膨成分，這種組成仍延續前次收盤對限制性實質利率的側重。5年期券領跌當日走勢，長債殖利率升幅則小於殖利率曲線腹部。投資人對政策限制的時點與持續性進行更強烈的重新定價，而非納入新的長期財政衝擊。

融資與信用是限制系統性解讀的主要因素。大規模3個月期及6個月期國庫券標售吸引大量投標，即期現金市場情勢仍然寬鬆，高收益上市基金的跌幅也遠小於科技股。不過，緩衝有限：財政赤字與利息支出強化長端市場出清的高門檻，AI 融資正轉向更複雜的結構，美元走強之際新興市場債務則轉弱。因此，收盤走勢結合通膨政策衝擊與有序去化；市場功能維持完整，但公共與私人借款人的存續期間及再融資成本均告上升。

## 美債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美債殖利率曲線全面走弱，其中5年期券表現落後。2年期殖利率上升5個基點至 4.26%，5年期上升7個基點至 4.37%，10 年期上升6個基點至 4.62%，30 年期則上升4個基點至 5.10%。相較前次最終收盤，此波走勢延續前端反轉，並使指標債與長債殖利率高於 7 月 10 日的水準。這種排序使升息迫近與長期維持限制性政策相互重疊的區段承受最大壓力；長債升幅較小，則顯示財政及期限溢價並未帶來當日最大的新增衝擊。

由於大部分調整由殖利率曲線腹部承擔，各段曲線斜率漲跌互見。2s10s 利差擴大1個基點至 36，2s30s 則縮窄1個基點至 84。2s5s10s 蝶式利差上升3個基點至-14，2s10s30s 蝶式利差也上升3個基點至-12。因此，單純以熊市趨平概括，會忽略壓力集中於5年期券的特徵。前端反映近期採取行動的可能性，殖利率曲線腹部納入更長期的限制性政策路徑，30 年期殖利率則是在起始水準本已偏高的情況下，單日升幅較小。

抗通膨美債將指標殖利率走勢拆分為占主導地位的實質利率成分，以及較小的通膨增量。10 年期實質殖利率上升4個基點至 2.36%，10 年期損益兩平通膨率則上升2個基點至 2.26%。5年期與7年期實質殖利率均上升4個基點，分別達到 2.06% 與 2.20%。20 年期與 30 年期實質殖利率上升3個基點，分別至 2.69% 與 2.89%。油價推升通膨疑慮，但隨政策緊縮成為近期實際可能，投資人也要求殖利率曲線各段提供更高的實質報酬。

Waller 建立了能源與實質利率之間的政策反應函數橋梁。他表示，若核心通膨再次偏熱，FOMC 可能必須考慮近期緊縮，並指出截至 5 月的1年內，核心 PCE 通膨已達 3.4%。他的擔憂不只涉及油價：關稅、能源與 AI 基礎設施需求都在加重壓力，而消費需求仍具韌性，勞動市場看來穩定。他還表示，需要數個月較低的數據才能確認情勢改善，因而提高單一良性數據逆轉重新定價的門檻。

這項訊息仍保留雙向界線。Waller 表示通膨預期穩固受控，勞動市場也不如 2021-22 通膨時期緊繃。核心通膨若持續放緩，仍可能支持維持利率不變。週一的賣壓反映下一份數據周邊的不對稱性：另一份堅挺數據可能啟動近期升息討論，但單月降溫未必足以判定基礎壓力是否正在消退。Warsh 首度以主席身分出席國會作證在即，也增添不確定性，因為他尚未提出詳盡的經濟觀點或利率預測。

國庫券殖利率曲線強化政策面的解讀，但未顯示眼前的融資壓力。4週期與6週期券的票息等值殖利率均上升1個基點至 3.70%，8週期券則持平於 3.72%。13 週期券上升5個基點至 3.85%，26 週期券上升4個基點至 4.00%，52 週期券則上升6個基點至 4.12%。3個月至1年期的重新定價幅度較大，符合預期政策路徑上移；最短天期波動溫和，則與近期現金充裕相符。

週一的標售顯示，現金仍足以去化大量國庫券供給。91 天期標售以 3.760%的最高利率接受 $920億，投標倍數為 2.84。間接投標人獲配已接受競爭性投標的 56.76%，直接投標人獲配 8.24%，交易商則為 34.99%。182 天期標售以 3.860% 接受 $790.01億，投標倍數為 3.11；間接投標占 66.49%，直接投標占 8.09%，交易商承接 25.42%。2檔證券均於 7 月 16 日發行及交割，使標售需求訊號與即將發生的現金移轉分開。

聯準會的準備金管理操作提供額外的融資面制衡。紐約聯準銀行在截至 8 月 13 日的每月期間內，將國庫券購買規模維持在約 $100億，並計畫進行約 $176億的再投資購買。7 月 8 日銀行準備金為 $3.14兆，在現金充裕的情況下，有擔保隔夜融資利率一直低於準備金餘額利率。這些條件說明，鷹派政策重新定價為何能與有序的貨幣市場並存。風險則位於後續：美國財政部發行國庫券並將現金餘額提高至預期超過 $1兆，將從銀行體系抽走準備金。

利率收盤走勢將貨幣政策風險與市場基礎運作區分開來。能源與 Waller 將預期緊縮時點提前，指標殖利率調整主要由實質殖利率上升帶動，殖利率曲線腹部則承受最大單日損失。國庫券需求與寬鬆的擔保融資環境，避免此波走勢演變成流動性事件。長端仍需提供可觀補償，但其相對韌性顯示，週一並非新一波由財政因素主導的陡峭化。政府最新公布的赤字與利息成本數據，稍後進一步強化了這項結構性負擔。

## 信用、融資與初級市場去化

上市信用資產隨美債殖利率上升而走弱，但低品質債券基金受創程度小於存續期間較長的投資等級債。LQD 下跌 0.47%，HYG 下跌 0.24%，JNK 下跌 0.18%。此一格局符合基礎利率急升與選擇性避險交易，而非資金全面撤出企業信用市場。初級市場仍可進行融資，但當日發行人動態顯示，隨指標殖利率及競爭性供給上升，投資人日益要求合適的結構、擔保品與補償。

AI 基礎設施仍是最大的結構性去化挑戰。超大規模雲端業者過去透過無擔保投資等級債券，為大部分資本支出融資，但投資人正接近單一發行人限額，並質疑該管道還能承載多少供給。資料中心債券將租戶的租金付款轉化為擔保品，可擴大融資基礎，但也使承銷重點轉向租約持久性、租戶支付能力、工程完工、電力取得及正常運轉時間。因此，超越超大規模雲端業者的企業資產負債表可擴大融資容量，代價則是更高的分析與定價門檻。

避險容量成長證實，投資人正在管理規模更大、分化程度更高的科技信用曝險。Royal Bank of Canada 已開始為美元單一名稱信用違約交換造市，並計畫擴展至歐元合約。該行表示，其 6 月單一名稱 CDS 成交量幾乎是年初至今成交量的4倍，信用指數選擇權成交量則較1年前增加1倍。發行人進入債券市場時建立的新交換及籃子工具，可以改善風險移轉；但市場對此類工具的需求，也反映投資人已較不願將所有大型 AI 借款人都視為可互換的高評級存續期間資產。

證券化提供另一條繞過無擔保企業資產負債表的途徑。Robinhood 正測試市場對至少 $4億、分為4部分的信用卡應收帳款債券之需求；潛在規模為 $5億，最高評級分券的初步利差討論接近指標利率加 80 個基點。Wells Fargo 與 Barclays 負責此交易，Coastal Community Bank 則持有相關品牌卡帳戶。年內信用卡擔保證券的募資額已超過 $110億，其中包括 Capital One的 $38.5億發行，顯示消費者應收帳款仍能吸引專門融資。

英鎊信用市場顯示，發行人可進入市場，但附帶個別條件。Pinewood 為可能發行的固定利率債券舉行投資人會議，其評級分歧：S&P 評為 BB-，Fitch 則評為 BBB-。其現有資本結構包括1檔 2030 年到期、票息 6%、規模 £7.5億的債券，以及1檔 2027 年 11 月到期、規模 £3億的債券。行銷流程顯示，即使利率走高且地緣政治引發賣壓，市場熟悉的營運資產仍可嘗試發債；但評級分歧與近期到期債務，使定價仍取決於品牌實力以外的信用分析。

信用市場以維持開放但分化加劇的狀態結束本交易日。美債使總報酬承壓，科技業借款同時催生替代結構及新的避險需求，證券化市場則提供傳統債券以外的融資容量。這些管道都未消除較高基準利率所帶來的根本限制，只是將其重新分配至無擔保發行人、專案與租戶風險、應收帳款池及特定到期結構。高收益債券基金跌幅溫和，使廣泛融資壓力仍受控制，即使融資架構日益複雜、去化新債的成本也告上升。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

日本逆轉了前次收盤對全球存續期間最具支撐力的訊號。先前要求公共退休基金增加國內投資的呼聲曾推升 JGB 與日圓，但週一報導指出，GPIF 並未計畫調整資產配置。日圓兌美元曾貶值 0.4% 至 162.36，JGB 跌勢擴大。這檔規模 $1.8兆的基金在 2025 年 3 月檢討時，仍將國內債券、國內股票、外國債券及外國股票各維持25%的配置。任何策略變更都必須經過正式的受託程序，降低政治指示資金迅速轉向國內存續期間資產的可能性。

這項逆轉重新開啟美債的2項傳導風險。若 GPIF 未迅速調整，日本長端仍將受到大規模公共支出、日圓疲弱，以及市場擔憂 BOJ 正常化落後於通膨等因素壓力。JGB 殖利率上升，會提高全球主權債券的相對報酬門檻。與此同時，維持配置不變，也避免基金立即調降外國資產目標。因此，近期影響是 JGB 下跌推升全球期限壓力，而日本海外投資需求的長期方向仍未確定。

歐洲呈現停滯性通膨組合，而非明確的存續期間訊號。荷姆茲海峽再度受阻，打擊能源供給預期，經濟學家將 2026 年歐元區成長預測中位數從 0.7% 下調至 0.5%，低於 ECB的 0.8% 基準預測。其通膨預測維持在 2.8%，遠高於 ECB 目標，並預期 9 月再次升息。成長轉弱通常會支撐主權債券存續期間，但能源驅動的通膨及進一步緊縮的可能性，使實質利率門檻維持高檔，也限制經濟活動放緩能傳導至美債的利多。

美國財政更新在美東時間下午 2 時公布後，強化了結構性供給背景。6 月錄得 $1,203億赤字，而1年前為 $270億盈餘，主因收入減少、支出增加。財政年度迄今赤字擴大至 $1.367兆，淨利息支出達 $8,272億，向公眾借款總額為 $1.34兆。這些數據公布時間太晚，無法解釋先前的利率賣壓，但確實在最後數小時交易中，強化長端市場出清利率所納入的主權融資規模與利息支出。

因此，全球存續期間透過不同機制傳導壓力。日本消除了買方基礎即時馳援的可能，歐洲同時面臨成長轉弱與高於目標的通膨，美國財政報告則凸顯持續的借款需求。美債殖利率曲線以腹部領跌，顯示 Fed 重新定價仍是當日首要驅動力。不過，30 年期殖利率高於 5%，反映在1個公共資產負債表、能源安全與國內退休基金任務競逐存續期間資本的世界裡，即使標售有序出清，長端仍需提供較高補償。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

相較前次收盤，新興市場債務面臨更嚴峻的共同背景。美國美元指數上升 0.31% 至 101.28，10 年期美債實質殖利率也上升，EMB 因而下跌 0.64%。美元與美國折現率同步上升，使硬通貨借款人的金融條件收緊，而油價衝擊則使進口國與出口國進一步分化。指數走弱並未抹去個別國家的支撐，但提高了財政可信度、準備資產深度及主動到期管理的價值。

印尼獲得評級面的緩解，但市場並未全面反轉。S&P 確認其主權評級為 BBB、展望穩定，理由包括 GDP 3%的赤字上限及一貫的財政紀律。Moody’s 與 Fitch 先前已將展望調降至負向，此決定消除了眼前的降評威脅。印尼 10 年期美元債仍下跌 0.4 美分至 100.9，本地債券則收復部分失地，股市收高。削減免費餐點預算的計畫支撐財政錨，但印尼盾年內貶值近 8%，使外部信心及輸入型通膨仍居核心地位。

智利顯示，利差偏緊可與更大的借款需求並存。其美元債利差自 3 月底以來已縮窄 24 個基點至 82，接近 2007 年最後1次出現的水準。參議院批准額外舉借 $62億，財政部則在原先尋求較計畫增加 36%的債務後，規劃於年內剩餘期間在國際市場發行 $52億。經濟活動已連續5個月較上年同期萎縮，結構性財政餘額目標也被放寬，強勁的市場融資能力正彌補財政滑落。幾乎完全依賴能源進口，使荷姆茲海峽衝擊成為額外的執行風險。

肯亞擬議的負債管理主要處理展期時點，而非舉債能力。官員正考慮在截至 2027 年 6 月的財政年度內，回購最多 $5億的歐洲債券，並透過新發美元債為回購融資。這將是2年內第4次類似操作。截至 3 月底，外債總額為 $437億，其中 $106億欠歐洲債券投資人，而債務償還支出已超過醫療與教育支出。延長到期日可平滑近期債務結構，但反覆交換使市場融資能力與收入動員仍是最具約束力的因素。

本地因應通膨的政策正變得更具限制性。衣索比亞將基準利率從 15% 上調至 16%，為該利率推出以來首次上調，同時取消銀行信貸年增率 24%的上限。5 月通膨已升至 13.4%，央行預期未來6個月仍將維持2位數。印度 6 月 CPI 從 3.93% 加速至 4.38%，部分由食品通膨從 4.78% 升至 5.32% 帶動；農村通膨為 4.74%，高於城市的 3.92%。儘管衣索比亞與印度的信用與成長結構不同，這些情況都使本地主權債殖利率獲得更堅實的下檔支撐。

奈及利亞也出現同樣能源與預期管道的前瞻版本。Standard Chartered 將 2026 年通膨預測從 12% 上調至 15.5%，並認為今年僅會降息 150 個基點，使政策利率從目前的 26.5% 降至 25%。央行的通膨感受指數從 44.8 升至 45，能源、治安不佳、借款成本及匯率均被列為主要壓力。這項預測並非政策決定，但顯示在通膨預期本已難以錨定的地區，燃料成本再度上升可能拖慢寬鬆步伐。

新興市場收盤走勢結合共同的美元利率衝擊，以及差異顯著的國內緩衝。印尼維持投資等級，智利保有異常低廉的外部融資管道，肯亞則試圖以較長期結構置換近期到期債務。衣索比亞採取緊縮，而印度通膨轉強及奈及利亞預測上修，則限制本地存續期間表現。因此，廣泛賣壓同時包含全球與主權因素：美國實質殖利率上升提高基礎成本，而各借款人的財政規則、能源曝險及再融資結構，決定此成本的傳導強度。

## 跨資產

油價是地緣政治向固定收益市場傳導的主要橋梁。新一波交火及宣布對通過荷姆茲海峽的貨物收取 20% 費用後，WTI 上漲 9.42% 至 $78.14，Brent 上漲 9.59% 至 $83.30。按當時價格估算，一艘滿載的超大型原油輪可能須支付約 $3,200萬，遠高於先前所述的伊朗通行費。此項提案如何實施尚不明朗，但其推高航運與燃料成本的可能性，已足以在週一交易期間重新引發通膨疑慮。

股票與波動率顯示，衝擊已傳至成長型資產，但未造成無差別拋售。S&P 500 下跌 0.79%，Nasdaq 100 下跌 1.88%，費城半導體指數下跌 4.78%。VIX 上升 14.17% 至 17.16。南韓晶片股的賣壓蔓延至美國交易，包括 SK Hynix的美國存託股票下跌 9.3%，使科技股承受較大損失。股票走弱與美債下跌的組合符合通膨型折現率衝擊，而半導體走勢則增添產業特定壓力。

美元與黃金強化論點中的實質利率面向。美元指數升至 101.28，增加新興市場借款人的外部融資負擔。即使地緣政治緊張加劇，黃金期貨仍下跌 2.61% 至 $3,997。較高的實質殖利率與較強的美元壓過黃金的避險功能，使本交易日有別於美債與黃金同步上漲的傳統恐慌型走勢。這波跌勢也發生在 Waller 警告核心通膨持續可能引發政策回應之後。

因此，跨資產走勢證實固定收益市場變動的2個面向。油價與波動率上升傳遞通膨衝擊，科技股走弱則顯示較高折現率與區域性 AI 壓力正在打擊風險資產。美元走強、黃金價格下跌，與實質殖利率上升一致。高收益信用資產跌幅遠小於半導體，波動率也仍低於危機水準，因此尚待釐清的問題是持續性：荷姆茲海峽成本衝擊若持久，可能擴大重新定價；若情勢正常化，則將揭示週一有多少走勢源自 Waller 對基礎通膨的擔憂。

## 前瞻市場意義

6 月 CPI 與實質所得將於 7 月 14 日美東時間上午 8:30 公布，主席 Kevin Warsh 隨後於上午 10:00 出席眾議院作證，構成政策路徑的即時考驗；7 月 15 日公布的 6 月 PPI 及 Warsh 出席參議院作證，則延續同一主線。核心與服務通膨若全面降溫、實質所得轉強，且主席願意強調耐心，將挑戰週一對 7 月升息機率的上調，並紓解2年至5年期券壓力。核心壓力若持續、實質所得成長疲弱，或明確展現緊縮準備，則會證實 Waller的警告：能源正在放大最新交火前便已存在的通膨問題。前端殖利率、實質利率、損益兩平通膨率、美元及事件波動率，將顯示此次收盤是持久的政策反應函數重設，還是1次仍待驗證的高信念走勢。

7 月 14 日美東時間上午 11:30的 $950億、42 天期國庫券標售，週一售出之 91 天期與 182 天期國庫券於 7 月 16 日交割，以及當日下午 4:30 公布的 H.4.1 流動性報告，共同構成現金去化進程。標售投標倍數強勁、非交易商需求廣泛、擔保融資穩定，且準備金在交割期間仍然充裕，將證實週一較長天期國庫券走弱反映的是政策預期。若需求偏重交易商、融資利率走高，或財政部重建現金餘額時造成準備金大幅流失，將使前端重新納入流動性溢價。Aquarion Water Authority 計畫於 7 月 14 日發行 $24億，則在同一去化主題下提供另一項公共信用觀察：其 A- 優先債與 BBB+ 次順位債的市場接受度，將顯示1個大型新市政發行人能否為已完成的收購融資，而不干擾原本有序的初級市場。

日本約 ¥7,000億的 20 年期 JGB 將於 7 月 14 日標售，是近期檢驗全球買方基礎逆轉最明確的考驗；7 月 15 日歐元區工業生產則提供歐洲存續期間訊號中的成長面向。JGB 需求強勁、標售後交易穩定及日圓走強，將挑戰週一市場對國內資金即時去化能力失去信心的情況，即使 GPIF的策略配置維持不變。投標疲弱、超長天期債券再度走弱或日圓進一步貶值，將證實財政供給與 BOJ 延後正常化仍要求較高的期限溢價，並透過相對價值及避險成本，使美債長天期殖利率保持敏感。歐洲產出疲弱可能增添存續期間支撐；若經濟展現韌性且能源通膨持續，則將維持較高的全球實質利率門檻，使美國長端即使在週一賣壓由殖利率曲線腹部領跌的情況下，仍繼續承壓。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為基準。

overall_summary.headline: 油價衝擊與 Fed 路徑收緊，引發以殖利率曲線腹部領跌的美債賣壓
overall_summary.session_narrative: 7 月 13 日紐約收盤延續限制性利率環境；油價再度引發整體通膨風險，Waller 也使近期升息成為實際可能，帶動5年期券領跌。名目利率的重新定價主要由實質殖利率上升帶動，美元走強及風險資產轉弱，則使壓力從美債擴散至其他市場。國庫券需求強勁、現金充裕，加上高收益債跌幅相對有限，使此波走勢未演變成流動性或系統性信用事件。
overall_summary.summary: 荷姆茲海峽風險再起，加上 Waller 對通膨提出警告，推升預期政策路徑，導致美債殖利率曲線全面走弱。漲勢主要由實質殖利率帶動，而國庫券需求強勁、融資情勢寬鬆，使市場運作維持有序。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤走勢反映政策路徑與實質利率衝擊，流動性仍正常運作。
section_summaries.next_check: 核心通膨與 Warsh的證詞必須顯示，緊縮重新定價能否持續。
section_summaries.summary: 荷姆茲海峽衝突再起，加上 Waller的警告，將能源衝擊轉化為更高的預期政策路徑，使美債損失集中於前端與殖利率曲線腹部。漲勢主要由實質殖利率帶動，而國庫券去化有序、高收益債受創有限，抑制了市場對系統性融資風險的擔憂。

section_summaries.market_read: 殖利率曲線腹部表現落後，顯示市場正在重設限制性政策路徑，而非出現財政因素主導的陡峭化。
section_summaries.next_check: 下一份核心通膨數據必須判定，升息討論是否可持續，而非僅是單日重新定價。
section_summaries.section_title: 美債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 近期升息風險與長期維持限制性政策的預期交會，使美債跌勢以5年期券最為顯著。實質殖利率的貢獻大於損益兩平通膨率，而國庫券標售強勁、準備金充裕，顯示鷹派政策重新定價尚未轉化為眼前的融資壓力。

section_summaries.market_read: 企業融資管道仍然暢通，但較高的基準利率正將風險重新分配至不同結構。
section_summaries.next_check: 新發無擔保債與證券化交易的市場接受度，必須顯示這種分化是否仍然有序。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場去化
section_summaries.summary: 信用市場仍保持開放，但分化加劇；美債殖利率上升對存續期間較長的投資等級債造成的壓力，大於高收益債。AI 融資轉向有擔保結構與新型避險工具，而證券化及選擇性發債在更高的承銷與定價門檻下維持了融資容量。

section_summaries.market_read: 即使單日損失由殖利率曲線腹部領跌，長端仍保有結構性供給溢價。
section_summaries.next_check: 日本 20 年期公債標售必須檢驗，國內需求能否抑制全球期限壓力。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 日本排除近期調整退休基金配置的可能、歐洲同時面臨成長轉弱與能源帶動的通膨，加上美國財政更新強化借款壓力，使全球存續期間失去支撐。Fed 重新定價仍是當日主要驅動力，但高檔的公共融資需求使長端市場出清門檻居高不下。

section_summaries.market_read: 新興市場存續期間目前同時承受更高的全球折現率與更嚴厲的國別風險懲罰。
section_summaries.next_check: 美元強勢、能源成本及再融資執行情況，必須揭示哪些主權緩衝能夠維持。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 美元走強與美國實質殖利率上升，使新興市場債務的金融條件收緊，油價衝擊則進一步拉大能源進口國與出口國的差距。印尼的財政錨、智利的市場融資能力及肯亞的負債管理提供緩衝，但再融資結構與本地通膨仍使各國主權債表現分化。

section_summaries.market_read: 跨資產走勢證實這是1場通膨型實質利率衝擊，而非傳統避險事件。
section_summaries.next_check: 荷姆茲海峽成本若持續，必須推動更廣泛的重新定價；若回歸正常，則將凸顯核心通膨因素。
section_summaries.summary: 油價將地緣政治風險傳導至通膨預期，科技股下跌及波動率上升則顯示折現率衝擊正擴散至風險資產。美元走強、黃金走弱，證實實質殖利率居於主導地位；不過，高收益債跌幅有限，波動率也低於危機水準，顯示市場並未出現無差別拋售。

section_summaries.market_read: 這波賣壓需要從政策預期、現金去化及全球存續期間三方面獲得確認。
section_summaries.next_check: 在預定考驗期間，關注2年至5年期券、擔保融資及長天期殖利率。
section_summaries.summary: 3項近期考驗將決定週一的重新定價能否持續：通膨數據與 Warsh 證詞、國庫券交割的流動性進程，以及日本長天期公債標售。關鍵在於政策、融資與全球期限溢價管道，是會驗證這波走勢，還是顯示這只是1次仍待確認的高信念收盤定價。

watch_events.market_implication: 通膨降溫將紓解前端壓力；數據堅挺則會驗證實質殖利率與美元走高，以及事件風險上升。
watch_events.market_trigger: 核心與服務通膨全面降溫並搭配耐心指引，或壓力持續且展現緊縮準備。
watch_events.title: 通膨數據與 Warsh 證詞
watch_events.watch_line: CPI、實質所得、PPI 與 Warsh的證詞將檢驗近期政策路徑。

watch_events.market_implication: 有序去化支持政策重新定價；若出現壓力，則將增加融資與公共信用溢價。
watch_events.market_trigger: 非交易商需求廣泛、融資穩定且 Aquarion 發債反應良好，或出現資產負債表承壓跡象。
watch_events.title: 現金與初級市場去化
watch_events.watch_line: 國庫券發行進程、準備金數據與 Aquarion 發債將檢驗現金及初級市場的去化能力。

watch_events.market_implication: 需求強勁將緩解期限壓力；需求疲弱則會透過相對價值，使美債長天期殖利率繼續承壓。
watch_events.market_trigger: JGB 需求強勁且日圓走穩，或投標疲弱、公債再度走弱且日圓進一步貶值。
watch_events.title: 日本長存續期間需求
watch_events.watch_line: 日本 20 年期公債標售與歐元區產出將檢驗買方需求及全球實質利率門檻。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The July 13 Treasury close extended the restrictive-rate regime from the prior final close, but energy returned as a direct catalyst after having receded from the July 10 move. Renewed US-Iran strikes and the proposed charge on cargo crossing the Strait of Hormuz revived the threat of a fuel-price shock. Christopher Waller then made that shock more potent by tying another hot core-inflation reading to possible near-term tightening. The 2-year yield closed at 4.26% and the 10-year at 4.62%, leaving the selloff anchored in a higher expected policy path rather than a flight to safety.

3 channels defined the move. Oil raised the prospective headline-inflation impulse and complicated the path back to price stability. Waller stressed that underlying inflation had already accelerated before the latest energy shock, so traders could not treat the move as a purely geopolitical premium. Money markets lifted the implied chance of a July rate increase to nearly 50%, from less than 40% earlier in the session, making the front end and belly especially sensitive to the next inflation release and Chair Kevin Warsh’s congressional testimony.

The nominal selloff also demanded more real compensation. The 10-year real yield finished at 2.36%, while inflation compensation rose by less than the nominal yield. That composition continued the prior close’s emphasis on restrictive real rates, even as the renewed oil shock added a clearer inflation component. The 5-year led the daily cheapening, and the long bond rose less than the belly. Investors were repricing the timing and persistence of policy restraint more forcefully than adding a fresh long-horizon fiscal shock.

Funding and credit supplied the principal limit to a systemic interpretation. Large 3- and 6-month bill auctions attracted substantial bids, immediate cash-market conditions remained soft, and high-yield listed funds fell much less than technology equities. Yet the cushion was thin: the fiscal deficit and interest bill reinforced the high long-end clearing threshold, AI financing was migrating toward more complex structures, and the dollar strengthened as emerging-market debt weakened. The close therefore combined an inflation-policy shock with orderly absorption, leaving market function intact while raising the cost of duration and refinancing across public and private borrowers.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasuries cheapened across the curve, with the 5-year sector underperforming. The 2-year yield rose 5 basis points to 4.26%, the 5-year added 7 to 4.37%, the 10-year increased 6 to 4.62%, and the 30-year rose 4 to 5.10%. Compared with the prior final close, the move extended the front-end reversal and pushed the benchmark and long bond above their July 10 levels. The ordering placed the greatest pressure where an imminent hike and a persistently restrictive path overlap, while the smaller long-bond move showed that fiscal and term premia did not deliver the day’s largest incremental shock.

Curve slopes were mixed because the belly bore much of the adjustment. The 2s10s spread widened 1 basis point to 36, whereas 2s30s narrowed 1 to 84. The 2s5s10s butterfly rose 3 basis points to -14, and the 2s10s30s butterfly also rose 3 to -12. A simple bear-flattening label therefore misses the 5-year concentration. The front end responded to the prospect of near-term action, the belly incorporated a longer restrictive path, and the 30-year carried a smaller daily increase from an already elevated starting yield.

Inflation-linked Treasuries divided the benchmark move into a dominant real-rate component and a smaller inflation increment. The 10-year real yield rose 4 basis points to 2.36%, while the 10-year breakeven increased 2 to 2.26%. 5- and 7-year real yields each gained 4 basis points, reaching 2.06% and 2.20%. 20- and 30-year real yields rose 3 basis points to 2.69% and 2.89%. Oil lifted inflation concern, but investors also required more real return across the curve as policy tightening became a live near-term possibility.

Waller supplied the reaction-function bridge between energy and real rates. He said another hot core reading could require the FOMC to consider tightening in the near term and noted that core PCE inflation had reached 3.4% in the year through May. His concern extended beyond oil: tariffs, energy and AI infrastructure demand were all contributing to pressure, while consumer demand remained resilient and the labor market appeared stable. He also said several months of lower readings would be needed to establish improvement, raising the bar for a single benign print to reverse the repricing.

The message retained a two-sided boundary. Waller described inflation expectations as well anchored and the labor market as less tight than during the 2021-22 inflation episode. A sustained moderation in core inflation could still justify holding rates steady. Monday’s selloff reflected the asymmetry around the next release: another firm reading could activate a near-term hike discussion, whereas one cooler month would not necessarily settle whether underlying pressure was receding. The approach of Warsh’s first congressional testimony as chair added uncertainty because he had not yet provided a detailed economic view or a rate projection.

The bill curve reinforced the policy interpretation without signaling immediate funding stress. 4- and 6-week coupon-equivalent yields each rose 1 basis point to 3.70%, and the 8-week yield held at 3.72%. The 13-week yield climbed 5 basis points to 3.85%, the 26-week added 4 to 4.00%, and the 52-week rose 6 to 4.12%. The larger repricing from 3 months to 1 year aligned with a higher expected policy path; the subdued movement at the shortest maturities was consistent with abundant near-term cash.

Monday’s auctions showed that cash could still absorb large bill supply. The 91-day sale accepted $92.000 billion at a 3.760% high rate and drew 2.84 times as many bids as awards. Indirect bidders received 56.76% of accepted competitive tenders, direct bidders 8.24%, and dealers 34.99%. The 182-day sale accepted $79.001 billion at 3.860%, with 3.11 coverage, a 66.49% indirect share, an 8.09% direct share, and 25.42% going to dealers. Both securities issue and settle on July 16, keeping the auction demand signal separate from the coming cash transfer.

Federal Reserve reserve-management operations offered an additional funding counterweight. The New York Fed kept bill purchases at about $10 billion for the monthly period ending August 13 and planned roughly $17.6 billion of reinvestment purchases. Bank reserves stood at $3.14 trillion on July 8, and secured overnight financing had been trading below the rate paid on reserve balances amid abundant cash. Those conditions explain why a hawkish policy repricing could coexist with orderly money markets. The vulnerability lies ahead as Treasury bill issuance and a cash balance expected to exceed $1 trillion withdraw reserves from the banking system.

The rates close separated monetary-policy risk from market plumbing. Energy and Waller pushed expected tightening forward, higher real yields carried most of the benchmark adjustment, and the belly absorbed the largest daily loss. Bill demand and soft secured funding prevented the move from becoming a liquidity event. The long end still required substantial compensation, but its relative resilience indicated that Monday was not a new fiscal-led steepening. That structural burden was reinforced later in the session by the government’s latest deficit and interest-cost figures.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Listed credit weakened alongside higher Treasury yields, though lower-quality funds showed less damage than duration-heavy investment grade. LQD fell 0.47%, HYG declined 0.24%, and JNK lost 0.18%. The pattern was compatible with a sharp rise in underlying rates and a selective risk-off session, not a uniform withdrawal from corporate credit. Primary-market activity remained available, but the day’s issuer developments showed that investors increasingly required the right structure, collateral and compensation as benchmark yields and competing supply rose.

AI infrastructure remained the largest structural absorption challenge. Hyperscalers had financed much of their capital spending through unsecured investment-grade bonds, yet investors were approaching single-issuer limits and questioning how much more supply that channel could carry. Data-center bonds broaden the funding base by converting tenant lease payments into collateral, but they shift underwriting toward lease durability, tenant payment capacity, construction completion, power access and uptime. Moving beyond the hyperscalers’ corporate balance sheets therefore expands capacity at a higher analytical and pricing hurdle.

The growth of hedging capacity confirmed that investors were managing a larger and more differentiated technology-credit exposure. Royal Bank of Canada had begun making markets in dollar single-name credit-default swaps and planned to expand into euro contracts. The bank said its June single-name CDS volume almost quadrupled its year-to-date volume, while credit-index option volume doubled from a year earlier. New swaps and baskets created as issuers enter the bond market can improve risk transfer, yet the demand for them also reflects a market less willing to treat every large AI borrower as interchangeable high-grade duration.

Securitization offered another route around unsecured corporate balance sheets. Robinhood was gauging demand for at least $400 million of credit-card receivables bonds in 4 parts, with potential size of $500 million and initial talk near 80 basis points over the benchmark for the highest-rated tranche. Wells Fargo and Barclays were running the transaction, while Coastal Community Bank owned the underlying branded-card accounts. More than $11 billion of card-backed securities had been raised during the year, including Capital One’s $3.85 billion sale, showing that consumer receivables still attracted dedicated financing.

Sterling credit illustrated access with issuer-level conditions attached. Pinewood held investor meetings for a possible fixed-rate bond, with ratings split between BB- at S&P and BBB- at Fitch. Its existing capital structure included a £750 million 6% note due in 2030 and a £300 million note maturing in November 2027. The marketing process showed that a familiar operating asset could approach the market even during a rates and geopolitical selloff, while the ratings split and near-term maturity kept pricing dependent on credit work beyond the strength of the brand.

Credit ended the session open but more segmented. Total returns were pressured by Treasuries, technology borrowing was generating both structural alternatives and new hedging demand, and securitized markets offered capacity beyond conventional bonds. None of those channels removed the underlying constraint from higher base rates. They redistributed it among unsecured issuers, project and tenant risk, receivable pools, and specific maturity profiles. The modest high-yield fund declines kept broad funding stress contained, even as the financing architecture became more complex and the cost of absorbing new debt rose.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

Japan reversed the prior close’s most supportive global-duration signal. The earlier call for public pensions to invest more at home had lifted JGBs and the yen, but Monday brought a report that no GPIF allocation overhaul was planned. The yen weakened as much as 0.4% to 162.36 per dollar and JGBs extended losses. The $1.8 trillion fund had retained equal quarter allocations to domestic bonds, domestic equities, foreign bonds and foreign equities at its March 2025 review. Any strategic change requires a formal fiduciary process, limiting the prospect of rapid politically directed demand for domestic duration.

The reversal reopened two transmission risks for Treasuries. Without a prompt GPIF shift, Japan’s long end still faces pressure from ambitious public spending, a weak currency and concern that BOJ normalization is lagging inflation. Higher JGB yields raise the relative return hurdle for global sovereign bonds. At the same time, leaving the allocation unchanged avoids an immediate reduction in the fund’s foreign-asset target. The near-term effect was therefore higher global term pressure through JGB losses, with the longer-run direction of Japanese overseas demand still unresolved.

Europe carried a stagflationary mix rather than a clean duration signal. Economists cut their median 2026 euro-area growth forecast to 0.5% from 0.7%, below the ECB’s 0.8% baseline, as renewed Hormuz disruption damaged expectations for energy supply. Their inflation forecast remained 2.8%, well above the ECB’s target, and they expected another rate increase in September. Weaker growth would ordinarily support sovereign duration, but energy-driven inflation and the prospect of additional tightening preserved a high real-rate hurdle and limited the relief that slower activity could transmit to Treasuries.

The US fiscal update strengthened the structural supply backdrop after its 2 p.m. ET release. June recorded a $120.3 billion deficit after a $27.0 billion surplus a year earlier, as receipts fell and outlays rose. The fiscal-year-to-date deficit widened to $1.367 trillion, net interest reached $827.2 billion, and borrowing from the public totaled $1.340 trillion. These figures arrived too late to explain the earlier rates selloff. They did reinforce the amount of sovereign funding and interest expense embedded in the long-end clearing rate during the final hours of trading.

Global duration consequently transmitted pressure through different mechanisms. Japan removed the prospect of an immediate buyer-base rescue, Europe paired weaker growth with above-target inflation, and the US fiscal statement underscored continuing borrowing needs. The Treasury curve’s belly-led move showed that Fed repricing remained the primary daily force. The 30-year yield above 5% nevertheless reflected a world in which public balance sheets, energy security and domestic pension mandates compete for duration capital even when auctions clear in an orderly way.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt faced a harsher common backdrop than at the prior close. EMB fell 0.64% as the US Dollar Index rose 0.31% to 101.28 and the 10-year Treasury real yield increased. The simultaneous rise in the dollar and US discount rate tightened conditions for hard-currency borrowers, while the oil shock divided importers from exporters. Index weakness did not erase country-specific support, but it increased the value of fiscal credibility, reserve depth and active maturity management.

Indonesia received rating relief without a full market reversal. S&P affirmed the sovereign at BBB with a stable outlook, citing the 3% of GDP deficit ceiling and a history of fiscal discipline. The decision removed an immediate downgrade threat after Moody’s and Fitch had moved their outlooks to negative. Indonesia’s 10-year dollar bond still traded 0.4 cents lower at 100.9, while local bonds recovered part of their losses and equities closed higher. Planned cuts to the free-meals budget supported the fiscal anchor, but a rupiah down almost 8% during the year kept external confidence and imported inflation central.

Chile showed how tight spreads can coexist with a larger borrowing requirement. Its dollar spread had narrowed 24 basis points since the end of March to 82, near levels last seen in 2007. The Senate authorized an additional $6.2 billion of borrowing, and the Finance Ministry planned $5.2 billion of international issuance over the rest of the year after seeking 36% more debt than initially planned. With economic activity contracting year over year for 5 months and the structural-balance target relaxed, strong access was compensating for fiscal slippage. Near-total dependence on imported energy made the Hormuz shock an added execution risk.

Kenya’s proposed liability management addressed rollover timing more than debt capacity. Officials were considering buying back as much as $500 million of eurobonds during the fiscal year ending June 2027 and financing the repurchase with new dollar debt. It would be the fourth such exercise in 2 years. External debt totaled $43.7 billion at the end of March, including $10.6 billion owed to eurobond investors, while debt service exceeded spending on health and education. Extending maturities can smooth the near-term profile, but repeated exchanges leave market access and revenue mobilization as the binding constraints.

Local policy responses to inflation were becoming more restrictive. Ethiopia raised its benchmark rate to 16% from 15%, the 1st increase since the rate was introduced, while removing a 24% annual cap on bank credit growth. Inflation had risen to 13.4% in May, and the central bank expected it to remain in double digits over the next 6 months. India’s June CPI accelerated to 4.38% from 3.93%, led partly by food inflation rising to 5.32% from 4.78%; rural inflation at 4.74% exceeded the 3.92% urban rate. Both cases put a firmer floor under local sovereign yields despite different credit and growth structures.

Nigeria added a prospective version of the same energy and expectations channel. Standard Chartered raised its 2026 inflation forecast to 15.5% from 12% and saw only 150 basis points of easing this year, which would leave the policy rate at 25% from 26.5% currently. The central bank’s inflation-perception index rose to 45 from 44.8, with energy, insecurity, borrowing costs and the exchange rate identified as key pressures. The forecast was not a policy decision, but it showed how renewed fuel costs could slow easing where expectations were already difficult to anchor.

The EM close combined a common dollar-rate shock with sharply different domestic buffers. Indonesia preserved investment grade, Chile retained unusually cheap external access, and Kenya sought to exchange near-term maturities for a longer profile. Ethiopia tightened, while firmer Indian inflation and revised Nigerian forecasts constrained local duration. The broad selloff therefore carried both global and sovereign content: higher US real yields raised the base cost, and each borrower’s fiscal rule, energy exposure and refinancing structure determined how strongly that cost transmitted.

## Cross asset

Oil was the principal bridge from geopolitics to fixed income. WTI rose 9.42% to $78.14 and Brent gained 9.59% to $83.30 after renewed strikes and the announcement of a 20% charge on cargo crossing Hormuz. At prevailing prices, the proposed payment was estimated at about $32 million for a fully loaded very large crude carrier, far above previously described Iranian tolls. The proposal’s implementation was unclear, but its potential to raise shipping and fuel costs was enough to revive inflation concern during Monday’s session.

Equities and volatility showed that the shock reached growth assets without producing indiscriminate liquidation. The S&P 500 fell 0.79%, the Nasdaq 100 declined 1.88%, and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index dropped 4.78%. The VIX rose 14.17% to 17.16. Technology bore the larger loss as the South Korean chip selloff spilled into US trading, including a 9.3% decline in SK Hynix’s American depositary shares. The combination of weaker stocks and cheaper Treasuries was consistent with an inflationary discount-rate shock, with the semiconductor move adding sector-specific pressure.

The dollar and gold reinforced the real-rate side of the thesis. The dollar index advanced to 101.28, increasing the external financing burden for emerging-market borrowers. Gold futures fell 2.61% to $3,997 even as geopolitical tension intensified. Higher real yields and a firmer dollar outweighed the metal’s haven role, distinguishing the session from a conventional fear-driven rally in both Treasuries and bullion. The decline also followed Waller’s warning that persistent core inflation could elicit a policy response.

Cross assets therefore confirmed both parts of the fixed-income move. Oil and rising volatility carried the inflation shock, while technology weakness showed that higher discount rates and regional AI stress were hitting risk assets. The stronger dollar and lower gold price aligned with higher real yields. High-yield credit declined far less than semiconductors and volatility remained below crisis levels, leaving the unresolved issue as persistence: a durable Hormuz cost shock could broaden the repricing, while normalization would expose how much of Monday’s move came from Waller’s underlying-inflation concern.

## Forward Market Significance

June CPI and real earnings at 8:30 a.m. ET on July 14, followed by Chair Kevin Warsh’s House testimony at 10:00 a.m., are the immediate test of the policy path; June PPI on July 15 and Warsh’s Senate appearance extend the same thread. Broad cooling in core and services inflation, firmer real earnings and a chair willing to emphasize patience would challenge Monday’s rise in July hike pricing and relieve the 2- to 5-year sector. Persistent core pressure, weak real-income growth or an explicit readiness to tighten would confirm Waller’s warning that energy is amplifying an inflation problem already present before the latest strikes. Front-end yields, real rates, breakevens, the dollar and event volatility will show whether the close was a durable reaction-function reset or a high-conviction move awaiting validation.

The $95 billion 42-day bill auction at 11:30 a.m. ET on July 14, the settlement of Monday’s 91- and 182-day sales on July 16, and the H.4.1 liquidity release at 4:30 p.m. that day form one cash-absorption sequence. Strong auction coverage, broad nondealer demand, stable secured financing and ample reserves through settlement would confirm that Monday’s outer-bill cheapening reflected policy expectations. Dealer-heavy demand, firmer funding rates or a material reserve drain as Treasury rebuilds its cash balance would reopen a liquidity premium at the front end. Aquarion Water Authority’s planned $2.4 billion sale on July 14 adds a separate public-credit observation within the same absorption theme: reception for its A- senior and BBB+ subordinate debt will show whether a large new municipal name can fund a completed acquisition without disturbing an otherwise orderly primary market.

Japan’s roughly ¥700 billion 20-year JGB auction on July 14 is the clearest near-term test of the global buyer-base reversal, with euro-area industrial production on July 15 providing the growth side of the European duration signal. Strong JGB demand, stable post-auction trading and a firmer yen would challenge Monday’s loss of confidence in immediate domestic absorption, even if GPIF’s strategic allocation remains unchanged. Weak bidding, renewed super-long cheapening or further yen depreciation would confirm that fiscal supply and delayed BOJ normalization still require a higher term premium, with Treasury long yields sensitive through relative value and hedging costs. Weak European output could add duration support; resilience alongside persistent energy inflation would preserve the higher global real-rate hurdle and leave the US long end exposed despite the belly-led nature of Monday’s selloff.

section_summaries.market_read: The close was a policy-path and real-rate shock, with liquidity still functioning normally.
section_summaries.next_check: Core inflation and Warsh’s testimony must show whether the tightening repricing can persist.
section_summaries.summary: Renewed Hormuz conflict and Waller’s warning converted an energy shock into a higher expected policy path, concentrating Treasury losses in the front end and belly. Real yields drove most of the move, while orderly bill absorption and limited high-yield damage contained systemic funding concerns.

section_summaries.market_read: Belly underperformance signals a restrictive-path reset rather than a fiscal-led steepening.
section_summaries.next_check: The next core print must distinguish a durable hike discussion from a 1-session repricing.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Treasury cheapening peaked in the 5-year sector as imminent-hike risk met expectations of prolonged restraint. Real yields contributed more than breakevens, while strong bill auctions and abundant reserves separated hawkish policy repricing from immediate funding stress.

section_summaries.market_read: Corporate funding remains available, but higher base rates are redistributing risk across structures.
section_summaries.next_check: Reception for new unsecured and securitized deals must show whether segmentation remains orderly.
section_summaries.summary: Credit stayed open but became more segmented as rising Treasury yields pressured duration-heavy investment grade more than high yield. AI financing shifted toward collateralized structures and new hedges, while securitization and selective issuance preserved capacity at higher underwriting and pricing hurdles.

section_summaries.market_read: The long end retains a structural supply premium even when daily losses are led by the belly.
section_summaries.next_check: Japan’s 20-year auction must test whether domestic demand can restrain global term pressure.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Global duration lost support as Japan ruled out an imminent pension-allocation shift, Europe combined weaker growth with energy-driven inflation, and the US fiscal update reinforced borrowing pressure. Fed repricing remained the day’s main force, but elevated public funding needs sustained the long-end clearing hurdle.

section_summaries.market_read: EM duration now carries both a higher global discount rate and sharper country-specific penalties.
section_summaries.next_check: Dollar strength, energy costs and refinancing execution must reveal which sovereign buffers hold.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: A firmer dollar and higher US real yields tightened conditions for emerging-market debt, with the oil shock further separating importers from exporters. Indonesia’s fiscal anchor, Chile’s market access and Kenya’s liability management offered buffers, but refinancing structures and local inflation kept sovereign outcomes differentiated.

section_summaries.market_read: Cross assets confirm an inflationary real-rate shock rather than a conventional haven episode.
section_summaries.next_check: Hormuz costs must either persist and broaden repricing or normalize and expose the core-inflation driver.
section_summaries.summary: Oil transmitted geopolitical risk into inflation expectations, while technology losses and higher volatility showed the discount-rate shock spreading through risk assets. A stronger dollar and weaker gold confirmed the dominance of real yields, yet modest high-yield losses and sub-crisis volatility argued against indiscriminate liquidation.

section_summaries.market_read: The selloff needs confirmation across policy expectations, cash absorption and global duration.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch the two-to-5-year sector, secured funding and long yields through the scheduled tests.
section_summaries.summary: 3 near-term tests will determine whether Monday’s repricing endures: inflation data and Warsh’s testimony, the bill-settlement liquidity sequence, and Japan’s long-bond auction. The key distinction is whether policy, funding and global term-premium channels validate the move or reveal a high-conviction close awaiting confirmation.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.36% (+4 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.26% (+2 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 36 bp (+1 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 84 bp (-1 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Oil shock and tighter Fed path drive a belly-led Treasury selloff
overall_summary.session_narrative: The July 13 NY close extended the restrictive-rate regime, with the 5-year sector leading losses as oil revived headline-inflation risk and Waller made near-term tightening a live possibility. Higher real yields carried most of the nominal repricing, while a firmer dollar and weaker risk assets spread the pressure beyond Treasuries. Robust bill demand, abundant cash and relatively modest high-yield losses kept the move from becoming a liquidity or systemic credit event.
overall_summary.summary: Treasuries cheapened across the curve as renewed Hormuz risk and Waller’s inflation warning raised the expected policy path. Higher real yields carried most of the move, while strong bill demand and soft funding kept market function orderly.

watch_events.market_implication: Cooling would relieve the front end; firmness would validate higher real yields, the dollar and event risk.
watch_events.market_trigger: Broad core and services cooling plus patient guidance, or persistent pressure and readiness to tighten.
watch_events.title: Inflation data and Warsh testimony
watch_events.watch_line: CPI, real earnings, PPI and Warsh’s testimony will test the near-term policy path.

watch_events.market_implication: Orderly absorption supports a policy repricing; strain would add funding and public-credit premia.
watch_events.market_trigger: Broad nondealer demand, stable funding and a well-received Aquarion sale, or signs of balance-sheet strain.
watch_events.title: Cash and primary-market absorption
watch_events.watch_line: The bill sequence, reserve data and Aquarion sale will test cash and primary-market absorption.

watch_events.market_implication: Strength would ease term pressure; weakness would keep Treasury long yields exposed through relative value.
watch_events.market_trigger: Strong JGB demand and a firmer yen, or weak bidding, renewed cheapening and further depreciation.
watch_events.title: Japan long-duration demand
watch_events.watch_line: Japan’s 20-year auction and euro-area output will test buyer demand and the global real-rate hurdle.
